Container.Name: PumpkinMC
Container.Overview:
Pumpkin is a high-performance Minecraft server software written entirely in Rust, supporting both Java and Bedrock Edition clients.
NOTE: Pumpkin is experimental software under active development and is not yet feature-complete. It is not recommended for production worlds. Always keep backups of your world data. The "latest" image tracks the most recent release build, so compatibility may change often.
Configuration files (config.toml, etc) are generated in the data folder on first launch and can be edited there.
PERMISSIONS: This image runs as the non-root user 2613:2613 and does not support PUID/PGID. If the server fails to start or save, run "chown -R 2613:2613 /mnt/user/appdata/pumpkinmc" from the Unraid terminal first.
TIP: If Crafty Controller or another Minecraft server is already using port 25565, change the host port here to something free (e.g. 30000).
Container.Changes: ### 2026-06-21 - Initial template for Pumpkin (Java & Bedrock). - Self-hosted icon, no-new-privileges hardening, optional Bedrock UDP port. - Added note about the container's non-root UID (2613) and appdata permissions.

Config.Name: Server Data
Config.Description: Pumpkin server data, world files, and config.

Config.Name: Java Minecraft Port
Config.Description: Java Edition server port. Change the host port if Crafty or another server already uses 25565.

Config.Name: Bedrock Port
Config.Description: Optional Bedrock Edition UDP port, used only if Bedrock support is enabled in config.toml.

Config.Name: RUST_LOG
Config.Description: Logging verbosity. Options: error, warn, info, debug, trace.
